answers.p4q8: Can't grasp domain, fix AI junk, or time crunch. Most hit inspection/verification walls.
answers.p4q10: Stewardship = guiding AI output you own. Accountability stays human, not team effort.
answers.p4q2: RQ1: when/how critical thinking happens with GenAI. RQ2: when/why effort changes. First=presence, second=effort.
answers.p4q4: Effort shifts from making stuff to judging AI output. Workers become stewards, not laborers.
answers.p4q6: Motivation wants critical thinking, ability blocks it. Breaks down when motivated but can't check facts or fix AI.

answers.p5q10: Explanations might blind people to bad AI. From finding they just raise blind acceptance.
answers.p5q2: Fugener: confidence cuts AI follow. Bansal/van der Waa: explanations boost acceptance even when wrong.
answers.p5q7: Boost AI acceptance or smart trust calibration. Acceptance had way more evidence.
answers.p5q9: XAI vs humans (XAI wins), XAI vs AI-only (tie). Can't claim explanations add value.
answers.p5q4: Tables: no XAI gain. Text: big gains. Text feels natural to humans.

answers.p6q9: Teachers should cut workload and deadlines since those drive ChatGPT use. Tell students to use it as a tool, not a shortcut.
answers.p6q4: 12 items → experts picked 10 → EFA dropped items 4&9 (low loadings). 8 items left with good stats.
answers.p6q2: Rewards worry either ups or cuts ChatGPT. Cuts won, scared kids avoid cheating risk.
answers.p6q7: Workload/time pressure mediated to all bad outcomes via ChatGPT. Rewards sensitivity only to procrastination/memory. Quality sensitivity did nothing.
answers.p6q10: Excess ChatGPT cuts brain work needed for memory, so students forget more. Less thinking = weaker recall.

finalResponses.fq_like_most_least: Some of the paper selection was a bit difficult
finalResponses.fq_missing_confusing_unnecessary: hard to go into detailed explanation of the word questions due to time constraints.

answers.cpq2: People switch from creating work to reviewing AI output. AI handles the basic production fast, so effort goes to checking instead.
answers.cpq4: Students use ChatGPT under deadline pressure, causing procrastination and memory loss. Shortcuts replace real learning.
answers.cpq6: High AI confidence = skip verification. Self-confidence = check more.
answers.cpq8: Workload pressure pushes quick ChatGPT use. University paper shows this creates shortcut habits.
answers.cpq10: AI boosts task scores (XAI/student papers) but reduces verification effort and increases blind reliance (critical thinking paper).
